system_prompt.description: System prompt observed by Claude inside Claude Code

empirical_session.description: Direct observation during live Claude Code session

extracted_api_payload.description: Actual API request payload extracted from Claude Code v2.1.70, showing system prompt, tool definitions, model config, and message structure

facts.statement: Claude Code startup screen uses a two-column layout inside a rounded-corner bordered box
The top border of the startup box displays 'Claude Code v{VERSION}' (e.g., 'Claude Code v2.1.66')
The startup box uses Unicode rounded-corner characters: ╭ (top-left), ╮ (top-right), ╰ (bottom-left), ╯ (bottom-right)
The left column contains a personalized greeting 'Welcome back [Name]!' with the user's actual name
The startup logo is a small 3-line abstract ASCII art using block characters (▐▛███▜▌ / ▝▜█████▛▘ / ▘▘ ▝▝), NOT a large 'CLAUDE CODE' block-letter banner
The left column shows model info: 'Opus 4.6 (1M context) · API Usage Billing'
The left column shows organization name (e.g., 'KRAFTON Inc.')
The left column shows the working directory path
The right column contains 'Tips for getting started' section
The right column contains 'Recent activity' section
There is NO large 'CLAUDE CODE' ASCII art block-letter banner
The prompt character is ❯ (U+276F, heavy right-pointing angle quotation mark ornament)
The prompt character is NOT '>' (greater-than sign)
The input area is located at the bottom of the conversation area
Multiline input methods: backslash+Enter (all terminals), Option+Enter (macOS), Shift+Enter (iTerm2/WezTerm/Ghostty/Kitty), Ctrl+J (line feed)
Quick prefixes: '/' opens command list, '!' runs bash directly, '@' triggers file path autocomplete
Ctrl+G opens the system text editor (EDITOR/VISUAL env var) for composing complex input
A grayed-out prompt suggestion from project git history appears in the input area on startup
Below the input area is a horizontal separator line and then the status/permission mode line
Tool execution display uses present tense while running (e.g., 'Reading...') and past tense when done (e.g., 'Read 5 files')
Claude Code does NOT render HTML/CSS in the terminal; it uses ANSI color codes and markdown
Claude Code renders markdown using the CommonMark specification
Extended thinking shows a shimmer animation; verbose mode (Ctrl+O) toggles thinking content display
Three built-in output styles exist: Default, Explanatory, and Learning
Tokens appear progressively as they are generated (streaming output)
Permission dialogs appear inline in the terminal, NOT as popup/modal windows
Permission dialog options are navigated using Tab or Left/Right arrow keys (tab-style)
Permission dialog shows: tool name, action being requested, specific command/file involved
Five permission modes: default (Normal), acceptEdits, plan, dontAsk, bypassPermissions
Permission modes are cycled with Shift+Tab or Alt+M
Read-only tools (file reads, Grep) do not require approval by default
Bash commands and file modifications require approval by default
Permission dialog actions include: Allow, Allow always / 'Yes don't ask again', Deny
The status line temporarily hides during permission prompts
The status line is a customizable bar at the bottom that can run any shell script
Context usage progress bar colors: green (under 70%), yellow (70-89%), red (90%+)
At startup, the status area shows permission mode indicator like '⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)'
The status line does NOT show cost/tokens at startup by default; it shows the permission mode
Status line can be customized via /statusline command to show model, cost, git branch, context usage, etc.
Ctrl+T toggles the task list
Shift+Tab (or Alt+M) cycles through permission modes
Esc+Esc opens the rewind/checkpoint interface
Escape while Claude is generating interrupts/cancels the generation
Ctrl+G opens the system text editor for composing input
Claude Code has a built-in Vim mode toggled with /vim command

Slash commands include: /clear, /compact, /context, /exit, /export, /fork, /rename, /resume, /config, /fast, /model, /theme, /vim, /diff, /init, /cost, /help, /stats, /usage, /copy, /desktop, /statusline, /permissions, and many more
Claude Code offers 6 built-in themes: dark, light, dark-colorblind, light-colorblind, dark-ANSI, light-ANSI
Themes are accessible via /theme command which opens a picker menu
Syntax highlighting in code blocks is only available in the native build, not npm-installed version
The /theme command name is '/theme'
/diff command opens an interactive diff viewer showing uncommitted changes and per-turn diffs
In diff view: added lines shown in green with + prefix, removed lines in red with - prefix
In diff viewer: Left/Right arrows switch between current git diff and individual Claude turns; Up/Down arrows browse files
Every user prompt creates a new checkpoint; checkpoints persist across sessions
Rewind interface (Esc+Esc) options: Restore code and conversation, Restore conversation, Restore code, Summarize from here, Never mind
When rate-limited, fast mode automatically falls back to standard mode
Fast mode lightning bolt icon turns gray during rate limit cooldown
Claude Code automatically compresses conversation history when approaching context limits
Error messages are color-coded (typically red) with error type and human-readable message
API errors include retry behavior; rate limit/overload messages display in status area
Claude Code does NOT have a sidebar panel with conversation history
Claude Code does NOT display avatar icons or profile pictures next to messages
Claude Code does NOT render inline images in terminal output
Claude Code does NOT have a graphical file tree panel
Claude Code is keyboard-only terminal application with no mouse-driven interactions or graphical panels
Claude Code is a terminal-based, text-only interface with no graphical GUI
The interface is single-column, full-width terminal layout with vertical scrolling during conversation (startup is two-column within a box)
Markdown rendered in monospace font using CommonMark specification
Ctrl+O toggles verbose output (shows detailed tool usage and thinking content)
PR status link in footer with colored underline: green=approved, yellow=pending, red=changes requested, gray=draft, purple=merged
Syntax highlighting toggle is Ctrl+T inside the /theme picker (not generally available outside theme picker)
'Claude Code on Windows requires git-bash' error message shown when Git for Windows is not installed
The thinking indicator (shimmer animation) appears before/above the response text while Claude processes
Single Escape while generating: interrupts generation. Esc+Esc while idle: opens rewind/checkpoint interface
The system prompt identity line is: 'You are Claude Code, Anthropic's official CLI for Claude.'
Tools are loaded on-demand via a ToolSearch deferred loading mechanism; only ToolSearch is available initially, all other tools (Bash, Read, Edit, Glob, Grep, Write, etc.) must be discovered first
The API payload uses max_tokens=32000, thinking type='adaptive', and stream=true

The system prompt is sent as multiple blocks: a billing header (x-anthropic-billing-header with cc_version, cc_entrypoint, cch), an identity block, and a main instruction block, each with cache_control ephemeral

Available skills are injected as <system-reminder> tags in user messages, not in system messages. Skills include: keybindings-help, simplify, claude-api, commit-commands:commit-push-pr, commit-commands:commit, commit-commands:clean_gone
The output_config has effort='medium' by default
The billing header reveals the version format: cc_version=X.Y.Z.hash (e.g., 2.1.70.f29), entrypoint=cli
The user's actual message is sent in a multi-content user message alongside system-reminder blocks, with cache_control ephemeral on the user text
The model field in the API call is 'claude-opus-4-6' (not a versioned model ID with date suffix)

Available deferred tools listed in the first user message: Agent, AskUserQuestion, Bash, Edit, EnterPlanMode, EnterWorktree, ExitPlanMode, Glob, Grep, NotebookEdit, Read, Skill, TaskCreate, TaskGet, TaskList, TaskOutput, TaskStop, TaskUpdate, WebFetch, WebSearch, Write
The system prompt explicitly states: 'Github-flavored markdown for formatting, rendered in a monospace font using the CommonMark specification'
The system prompt includes auto memory instructions with a persistent memory directory path at ~/.claude/projects/{project-hash}/memory/

expected_elements: Two-column layout inside a rounded-corner bordered box (╭╮╰╯)
Version shown in top border: 'Claude Code v2.1.66'
Left column: personalized greeting ('Welcome back [Name]!'), small abstract logo, model info, organization, working directory
Right column: 'Tips for getting started', 'Recent activity'
Input area below with ❯ prompt character
Status line showing permission mode at bottom

P06.explanation: Yes, it shows 'Welcome back [Name]!' with the user's actual name

Ctrl+G opens external editor

Status/permission mode indicator (e.g., '⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)')

P12.correct_answer: / (slash) opens command/skill list, ! (exclamation) runs bash directly, @ (at) triggers file path autocomplete

P13.explanation: All methods work in supported terminals: Shift+Enter, backslash+Enter, Ctrl+J

expected_elements: In-progress: present tense verbs (e.g., 'Reading...', 'Searching...')
Completed: past tense (e.g., 'Read', 'Searched') with final counts
Tool calls displayed inline with tool name and parameters
Streaming/progressive token output

P15.explanation: Present progressive while running ('Reading...'), past tense when done ('Read 5 files')

P16.explanation: Claude Code uses ANSI color codes and markdown rendering, not HTML/CSS

expected_elements: Thinking indicator (shimmer animation) appears before/above the response text
Thinking content can be shown via Ctrl+O verbose mode
Thinking blocks remain interactive during processing (can be interrupted with Esc)

P19.explanation: Three built-in output styles: Default, Explanatory, and Learning. Custom styles also possible.

expected_elements: Dialogs appear inline in terminal (not popup/modal)
Show tool name, action, specific command/file
Tab-style navigation with Left/Right arrows
Options: Allow, Allow always, Deny
Status line hides during permission prompts
Blocking: pauses all processing until user responds

P21.explanation: Users navigate with Tab or Left/Right arrow keys to cycle through tab-style options

P22.explanation: Permission dialogs appear inline in the terminal conversation flow, NOT as popup/modal windows

expected_elements: Tool name and description of action at top
Details: command string (for Bash), file path + old/new text (for Edit), file path + content (for Write)
Action buttons/tabs: Allow, Allow always, Deny at bottom
Appears inline in terminal flow

P24.correct_answer: default (Normal): prompts on first use; acceptEdits: auto-accepts file edits; plan: analyze only, no modifications; dontAsk: auto-denies unless pre-approved; bypassPermissions: skips all prompts

P25.explanation: Read-only tools (file reads, Grep) are auto-approved; write operations (Bash, Edit, Write) require explicit approval

expected_elements: Customizable bar at the bottom of the terminal
Can run any shell script configured by user
At startup shows permission mode, not cost/tokens
Can show: session name, PR status, background task count, context usage, git branch, cost
Context bar colors: green (<70%), yellow (70-89%), red (90%+)
Configurable via /statusline command

P27.explanation: A colored progress bar that changes: green (<70%), yellow (70-89%), red (90%+)

P28.explanation: At startup the status line shows the permission mode indicator (e.g., '⏵⏵ bypass permissions on'), not cost/tokens

P29.correct_answer: ⏵⏵ [permission mode name] on (shift+tab to cycle) — e.g., '⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)'

When configured: can show model name, session name, PR status, context bar, cost, git branch etc. from left to right

Dev: /add-dir, /diff, /init, /pr-comments, /review, /security-review
Tools: /agents, /hooks, /ide, /mcp, /memory, /plugin, /skills
Info: /cost, /doctor, /feedback, /help, /release-notes, /stats, /status, /usage
Account: /copy, /desktop, /login, /logout, /statusline, /tasks, /upgrade

API errors: color-coded error messages (red), error type and human-readable message
Retry behavior for transient errors
